text:
There once was a hummingbird. Life for this hummingbird was quite busy. Every morning, she would zoom about to discover the tasty insects to fuel her activity. After collecting nectar at lovely blooms, she'd hover among the foliage, gathering sustenance. Evening would find her perched on a branch, sun-kissed.

Once upon a time, in a grove not far away, a snake decided to slither into her sanctuary. She froze, her tiny heart pounding. The snake coiled around her branch. She closed her delicate wings. "Well," the snake mused, "you could use those feathers better."

She shook her head, her eyes narrowing. "You have no idea how many insects I've eaten this hour."

"Well, I'm just curious," the snake replied lazily.

Soon, something shiny caught her eye. A bead. It twinkled like a thousand dreams. Taking a cautious nibble, she popped it into her mouth. It was good.
